mission.title: dapr: Implement actor APIs for gRPC
mission.description: Implement actor APIs for gRPC. Requested by 36+ users.

steps.title: Check current dapr deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dapr version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
helm list -n dapr 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dapr installation.

steps.title: Review dapr configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dapr configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get configmap -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dapr
```
Today, the actor functionality (invoking actors, reminders, timers and receiving callbacks from the Dapr runtime) is only possible using HTTP.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Implement actor APIs for gRPC
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get events -n dapr --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Implement actor APIs for gRPC" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: If this issue is still relevant, I'd like to take a stab at this. Thanks

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dapr installed or the issue environment reproducible.
